name: Physicians & Surgeons
whatYouDo: Diagnose and treat illness and injury, perform exams, prescribe treatment, and (for surgeons) operate. The highest-paid broad occupation BLS tracks; specialty and setting drive most of the spread.
incomeRangeNote: BLS reports median 'equal to or greater than $239,200' (the OEWS top-code); many specialties (cardiology, ortho, anesthesiology) clear $400K-$600K+. Primary care lower end.
frequency: ~839,000 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's + MD/DO + residency (and often fellowship): ~11-15 yrs post-high-school; USMLE/COMLEX licensure

name: Airline Pilots (Airline, Copilots & Flight Engineers)
whatYouDo: Fly scheduled passenger and cargo aircraft for airlines, manage flight systems, navigation, and crew. Seniority and aircraft type drive pay; major-carrier captains earn well above the median.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $226,600 for airline pilots/copilots/flight engineers (May 2024); senior wide-body captains at majors exceed $400K. Commercial (non-airline) pilots are a separate, lower category (~$122,670 median).
frequency: ~100,000 employed as airline pilots/copilots/flight engineers (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's typically + FAA commercial + ATP certificate, 1,500 flight hours; ~$100K+ in flight training

name: Dentists
whatYouDo: Diagnose and treat problems with teeth and gums, perform procedures, and oversee dental staff. Many own or buy into a practice, which lifts income above the employee median.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $179,210 (May 2024); p10 < $84,740, p90 > $239,200. Practice owners and specialists (oral surgeons, orthodontists) substantially higher.
frequency: ~149,300 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's + DDS/DMD (~4 yrs dental school) + state licensure; ~8 yrs post-high-school

name: Financial Managers
whatYouDo: Direct an organization's financial health: produce reports, steer investment and capital decisions, and build long-term financial strategy. Includes controllers, treasurers, and finance directors.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $161,700 (May 2024); top decile and senior corporate roles (CFO-track, large firms) well into the $300K+ range with bonuses.
frequency: ~868,600 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's in finance/accounting/business + typically 5+ yrs experience; MBA/CPA/CFA common for advancement

name: Lawyers
whatYouDo: Advise and represent clients in legal matters, draft documents, negotiate, and argue cases. BigLaw and specialized practice (corporate, IP, litigation) sit far above the median.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $151,160 (May 2024); bimodal market: large-firm associates start ~$225K while many public-sector/small-firm lawyers earn well under the median.
frequency: ~864,800 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's + JD (3 yrs law school) + pass the bar; ~7 yrs post-high-school

name: AI/ML Engineers (Computer & Information Research Scientists)
whatYouDo: Invent and improve computing approaches, including AI and machine-learning algorithms and systems. The BLS category that most closely tracks research-grade AI/ML roles; demand driven by generative AI adoption.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $140,910 (May 2024) for the occupation; brand-name AI labs and FAANG ML roles pay total comp far above this (often $300K-$700K+), but that is not the BLS median.
frequency: ~40,300 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Usually master's or PhD in CS/related; some research roles entered with a strong bachelor's; ~6-9 yrs

name: Petroleum Engineers
whatYouDo: Design and develop methods to extract oil and gas from below the earth, optimize drilling and recovery. Among the highest-paid engineering disciplines, concentrated in energy regions (TX, etc.).
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $141,280 (May 2024); among the top-paying engineering fields. Pay tied to commodity cycles and operator vs. service-company roles.
frequency: ~19,600 employed (BLS OOH, 2024), small, specialized field
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's in petroleum or related engineering; ~4 yrs; PE license for senior roles

name: Pharmacists
whatYouDo: Dispense prescription medications, advise patients and providers on safe use, and manage drug therapy. Retail employment has softened while hospital/clinical roles grow.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $137,480 (May 2024); fairly compressed range. Clinical/specialty and management roles toward the top.
frequency: ~335,100 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: PharmD (~4 yrs after 2+ yrs pre-pharmacy) + licensure (NAPLEX); ~6-8 yrs post-high-school

name: Aerospace Engineers
whatYouDo: Design aircraft, spacecraft, satellites, and missiles, and test prototypes. Work spans defense, commercial aviation, and the growing commercial-space sector.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $134,830 (May 2024); defense/space-prime and senior systems roles toward the top decile.
frequency: ~71,600 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's in aerospace or related engineering; ~4 yrs; some defense roles need a security clearance

name: Physician Assistants
whatYouDo: Examine, diagnose, and treat patients and prescribe medication, working on healthcare teams. Faster on-ramp than physician for a six-figure clinical income.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $133,260 (May 2024); surgical and high-acuity specialties and overtime push the top decile higher.
frequency: ~162,700 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's + master's PA program (~2-3 yrs) + PANCE licensure; ~6-7 yrs post-high-school

name: Software Developers
whatYouDo: Design, build, and maintain software applications and systems. The largest high-income tech occupation; comp scales sharply with company tier and seniority.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $133,080 (May 2024) for software developers; big-tech total comp (base+stock+bonus) commonly $250K-$500K+ at senior levels, well above the BLS median.
frequency: ~1.7M employed (developers, QA analysts & testers; BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's in CS/related typical; self-taught/bootcamp paths exist; ~$0-low capital, laptop

name: Nurse Practitioners (incl. Nurse Anesthetists & Midwives)
whatYouDo: Provide advanced primary and specialty care: assess, diagnose, prescribe, and (CRNAs) administer anesthesia. One of the fastest-growing occupations in the country.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $132,050 (May 2024) for the combined category; nurse anesthetists (CRNAs) earn well above $200K and pull the top of the range up.
frequency: ~382,700 employed (nurse anesthetists, midwives & NPs; BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: RN (BSN) + master's/DNP (APRN) + national certification & state licensure; ~6-8 yrs

name: Actuaries
whatYouDo: Use math, statistics, and financial theory to price and manage risk for insurers and pensions. Pay rises with each professional exam passed.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $125,770 (May 2024); fully credentialed Fellows (FSA/FCAS) and chief actuaries earn well into the $200K-$300K+ range.
frequency: ~33,600 employed (BLS OOH, 2024), small, credential-gated field
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's (math/stats/actuarial) + multi-year exam sequence (ASA/FSA or ACAS/FCAS); ~4 yrs to entry

name: Data Scientists
whatYouDo: Collect, model, and analyze large datasets to extract insights and build predictive systems. Fast-growing mathematical-science occupation feeding the AI/analytics boom.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $112,590 (May 2024); senior and big-tech data scientists' total comp ranges far higher (~$200K-$400K+), above the BLS median.
frequency: ~245,900 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's minimum, master's common; strong stats/programming; ~4-6 yrs

name: Financial & Investment Analysts (incl. Quants)
whatYouDo: Evaluate investments, securities, and financial data to guide buy/sell and capital decisions for banks, funds, and corporates. Quant and sell-side roles in major hubs pay far above the broad median.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $101,350 (May 2024) for financial & investment analysts; investment-banking and hedge-fund/quant roles with bonus run multiples higher but sit in the top tail, not the median.
frequency: ~368,500 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Bachelor's in finance/economics/math; CFA and (for quants) advanced quantitative degrees common; ~4 yrs

name: Electrician
whatYouDo: Install, maintain, and repair electrical wiring, panels, and systems in homes, businesses, and industrial sites; read blueprints, pull permits, and pass inspections. As an owner you bid jobs, run a crew, and add high-margin lines like solar and EV charging.
incomeRangeNote: Employee (BLS May 2024): median $62,350; p10 $39,430 .. p90 $106,030. Owner-operator: solo shop ~$70K-$130K; multi-crew $150K-$250K+; residential-only ceiling ~$130K-$150K, commercial/solar pushes higher.
frequency: ~818,700 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: 4-5 yr paid apprenticeship (~8,000 hrs) + journeyman license; master/contractor license to own a shop. Shop startup ~$10K-$75K (van, tools, license, insurance, bond).

name: Plumber / Pipefitter
whatYouDo: Install and repair water, gas, drainage, and heating pipe systems; diagnose leaks, set fixtures, and handle code inspections. As an owner you run service trucks, do emergency callouts at premium rates, and chase commercial/new-construction contracts.
incomeRangeNote: Employee (BLS May 2024): median $62,970; p10 $40,670 .. p90 $105,150. Owner-operator: solo ~$40K-$70K early, established solo $70K-$200K; multi-crew firms net $150K-$250K+ to owner.
frequency: ~504,500 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: 4-5 yr paid apprenticeship + journeyman then master plumber license (required to own a shop in most states). Startup ~$10K-$50K for a one-truck service operation.

name: HVAC Technician
whatYouDo: Install, service, and repair heating, ventilation, air-conditioning, and refrigeration systems; charge refrigerant (EPA 608 cert required), troubleshoot controls, and do seasonal maintenance contracts. As an owner you run service trucks, sell install jobs, and bank recurring maintenance revenue.
incomeRangeNote: Employee (BLS May 2024): median $59,810; p10 $39,130 .. p90 $91,020. Owner-operator: most owners $70K-$150K, single-truck modest, top multi-crew owners $200K-$500K+. Headline averages (~$86K) are noisy/self-reported.
frequency: ~425,200 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: 6mo-2yr trade school or 3-5yr apprenticeship + EPA 608 cert; state HVAC contractor license to own a business. Startup ~$10K-$60K (truck, tools, gauges, license).

name: Welder / Fabricator
whatYouDo: Join metal with arc, MIG, TIG, or stick welding for construction, manufacturing, pipelines, and ships; read fab drawings and pass certified weld tests. Specialize (pipeline, rig, underwater) or own a fab/mobile-rig shop to clear six figures.
incomeRangeNote: Employee (BLS May 2024): median $51,000; p10 $38,130 .. p90 $75,850. Specialists run far higher: pipeline $90K-$130K, rig welders $70K-$180K w/ OT, saturation/underwater $100K-$300K+. Owner-operator fab/rig shop ~$100K-$250K established.
frequency: ~457,300 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: 6mo-2yr welding program or apprenticeship + AWS/process certifications; no license to start a shop but rig welder needs a ~$40K-$100K welding truck/rig. Underwater adds commercial dive school ($15K-$30K).

name: Elevator / Escalator Installer
whatYouDo: Install, maintain, and modernize elevators, escalators, and moving walkways; troubleshoot hydraulics, cables, and electronic controls. Highly unionized (IUEC) with strong service contracts; one of the highest-paid trades that needs no degree.
incomeRangeNote: Employee (BLS May 2024): median $106,580; p10 $54,720 .. p90 $149,250. Mostly union employees of OEMs/service firms (Otis, KONE, Schindler) rather than owner-operators; service-route mechanics top out near six figures + OT and on-call.
frequency: ~24,200 employed (BLS OOH, 2024), small, hard-to-enter field
barrierToEntry: Competitive 4-5 yr NEIEP/IUEC apprenticeship (very limited slots) + license/exam in many states. Owner-operator path is rare; capital is the OEM relationship, not a shop.

name: Electrical Power-Line Installer (Lineman)
whatYouDo: Build and repair the high-voltage transmission and distribution grid; climb poles and towers, splice conductors, and restore power after storms. Travel/storm work and overtime drive earnings; grid-buildout and electrification keep demand strong.
incomeRangeNote: Employee (BLS May 2024): median $92,560; p10 $50,020 .. p90 $126,610. Storm/travel linemen and journeymen with heavy OT frequently clear $120K-$150K+. Mostly utility/contractor employees, not owner-operators.
frequency: ~127,400 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Lineman school (15wk-15mo) or 3-4 yr paid apprenticeship + CDL and pole/climbing certs. Physically demanding, high-hazard. Owner path is running a line-construction contracting crew, capital-heavy.

name: Construction Manager / General Contractor
whatYouDo: Plan, budget, schedule, and supervise construction projects from bid to handoff; coordinate subs, inspections, and clients. As a GC/owner you carry the contract, mark up subcontracted work, and take project profit on top of a manager's salary.
incomeRangeNote: Employee (BLS May 2024): median $106,980; p10 ~$67K .. p90 $164K+. Self-employed GCs/owners vary widely with project volume and margin, small-builder owners commonly $100K-$250K, larger firm owners well into six/seven figures from project profit.
frequency: ~550,300 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Often a bachelor's (construction mgmt/engineering) OR years on the tools rising to super; state GC license + bonding/insurance to own a firm. Capital to float payroll and materials between draws is the real gate.

name: Dental Hygienist
whatYouDo: Clean teeth, take x-rays, screen for oral disease, and educate patients in a dental office; mostly W-2 in private practices. High pay-per-hour with limited ownership upside, corporate-practice laws bar hygienists from owning full dental practices in most states.
incomeRangeNote: Employee (BLS May 2024): median $94,260; p10 $66,470 .. p90 $120,060. Owner upside is largely blocked: most states reserve practice ownership for licensed dentists. Independent paths exist only in a few states (CO proprietorship, ME IPDH, CA RDHAP) or via an MSO/staffing model.
frequency: ~221,600 employed (BLS OOH, 2024)
barrierToEntry: Associate degree (2-3 yr accredited program) + national/regional boards + state license. Low capital, but the employee-vs-owner ceiling is a legal wall, not a money wall.

name: Commercial Driver / Trucking Owner-Operator
whatYouDo: Haul freight long-haul or regional in a tractor-trailer on a CDL. As a company driver you're paid by mile/hour; as an owner-operator you own (or lease) the truck, run your own authority or lease to a carrier, and keep the margin after fuel, maintenance, and insurance.
incomeRangeNote: Company driver (BLS May 2024): median $57,440; p10 $38,640 .. p90 $78,800; most $55K-$85K. Owner-operator: grosses $150K-$250K but NETS ~$60K-$120K after expenses; paid-off truck can net $120K-$150K+. Caution: ~85-90% of new owner-operators fail within 2 yrs on cash flow.
frequency: ~2.2M heavy/tractor-trailer drivers (BLS, 2023-24)
barrierToEntry: CDL via 3-7 wk school, ~$4K-$12K (often carrier-paid). Owner-operator adds a truck ($30K used to $150K+ new) plus authority, insurance, and working capital.

name: Locksmith
whatYouDo: Install, rekey, and repair locks; cut keys, open lockouts, and service safes, automotive transponders, and commercial access control. Low overhead and high emergency-callout pricing make the solo owner-operator the default, most income beyond BLS is self-employed.
incomeRangeNote: Employee (BLS, recent OEWS): median ~$51,320; range ~$34,750 entry to $79,340+ for master/specialized. Owner-operator: ~$45,000-$100,000+, driven by emergency/after-hours premiums and automotive/commercial work. BLS excludes self-employed, so real owner pay sits above the median.
frequency: ~15,000 employed as W-2 (BLS, 49-9094), most working locksmiths are self-employed and uncounted
barrierToEntry: Short training (weeks to months) + state license/bonding where required (varies). Very low capital: ~$5K-$20K for tools, key machine, and a service vehicle. Among the lowest barriers in the trades.

name: Appliance Repair Technician
whatYouDo: Diagnose and fix washers, dryers, refrigerators, ovens, and dishwashers in customers' homes; order parts and bill per service call. Low barrier and strong solo-owner economics, a one-van operator keeps the full callout and parts markup.
incomeRangeNote: Employee (BLS OEWS, ~May 2024): median/average ~$50,640 (avg hourly $24.35); top earners ~$70K+. Owner-operator: single-tech owners commonly ~$75K, scaling to $150K-$200K+ EBITDA with 3-5 techs; multi-van firms much higher. BLS wage figure excludes self-employed.
frequency: ~29,950 employed as W-2 (BLS OEWS, 49-9031), many more are self-employed
barrierToEntry: Short, weeks to a few months of training or manufacturer certs (EPA 608 for sealed-system work); no universal license. Startup low: ~$10K-$75K single-tech (van, tools, parts, marketing); ~$100K+ for a storefront.

name: Chick-fil-A Operator
whatYouDo: Win a highly selective franchise slot, put up only a ~$10K franchise fee, and run a single high-volume QSR location hands-on. Chick-fil-A owns the real estate and equipment; you operate and take a cut of cash flow.
incomeRangeNote: Operators net roughly 5-7% of revenue. 2024 non-mall median AUV $9.227M and mean $9.317M (FDD Item 19) implies ~$465K-$651K on a freestanding store; mall stores (~$4.5M AUV) imply ~$225K-$315K. Median ~$240K-$300K after a manager wage is paid. Item 19 discloses sales, NOT profit, so income is third-party estimate.
frequency: ~3,000 US restaurants; only ~2,179 non-mall franchised units in the 2024 Item 19 base. Acceptance rate well under 1% of applicants.
barrierToEntry: $10K franchise fee + selection process; near-zero financial barrier but acceptance is the bottleneck. Must operate full-time, single unit, no absentee ownership.

name: Crumbl Cookies Franchise
whatYouDo: Buy a Crumbl franchise, build out a cookie shop, and run rotating weekly-menu retail with heavy social-media demand. You own the buildout and absorb the volatility.
incomeRangeNote: 2024 FDD Item 19: AUV ~$1.4M; MEAN net profit $251,706 but MEDIAN net profit only $77,359 (down 32% YoY). 43% of stores beat the mean; top store $600,554. Wide, right-skewed distribution -- median is the honest owner number. Base: 858 franchised units open all of 2024.
frequency: >1,000 US locations (rapid expansion 2018-2023, growth deliberately slowed in 2024).
barrierToEntry: Total investment ~$460K-$1.26M (FDD Item 7); real capital + buildout required.

name: Servpro Restoration Franchise
whatYouDo: Run a fire/water-damage cleanup and restoration franchise -- dispatch crews to insurance-paid jobs. Recurring, non-discretionary demand (disasters happen regardless of economy).
incomeRangeNote: Servpro makes NO Item 19 representation, so all figures are third-party estimates: ~$1.69M average gross sales and ~$203K-$254K estimated owner earnings. No median disclosed; treat the income figure as an estimate of a mid-range owner.
frequency: ~2,000+ US franchise territories (largest restoration franchisor).
barrierToEntry: Total investment roughly $200K-$250K + restoration training/certifications; operational know-how is the real barrier.

name: Two Men and a Truck Franchise
whatYouDo: Operate a local moving-company franchise -- trucks, movers, and dispatch. Build route density and crew utilization in a metro territory.
incomeRangeNote: 2024 FDD Item 19 average gross sales ~$2.36M per franchise (some sources cite $2.31M-$3.09M across single vs multi-unit). Item 19 discloses revenue, not profit; at typical ~7-10% owner margins, income ~$165K-$230K. Income figure is an estimate.
frequency: ~350+ US franchise locations.
barrierToEntry: Total investment ~$165K-$538K (FDD Item 7); trucks + working capital + crew.

name: Laundromat
whatYouDo: Own a coin/card self-service laundry -- a semi-absentee real-asset business. Buy or build, install machines, collect on utilities-driven margins with minimal labor.
incomeRangeNote: Typical store grosses ~$150K/yr (range $30K-$1M+) at 20-35% net margin -> owner income commonly $30K-$100K for a single store, scaling to ~$300K-$500K for larger/multi-store operators. Median single-store owner ~$60K-$90K. Figures are industry estimates, not a public registry.
frequency: ~18,374 laundromat businesses in the US (IBISWorld), declining ~-0.5%/yr 2020-2025; ~$7.2B industry.
barrierToEntry: $200K-$1M+ to buy or build out (equipment + lease/real estate); capital-heavy, semi-passive.

name: Self-Storage Facility
whatYouDo: Own and operate a self-storage facility -- a real-estate cash-flow play. Rent units, automate access/payments, run lean on labor; value rises with NOI.
incomeRangeNote: Industry-cited average annual owner profit/NOI ~$184,500; range $100K-$1M+ depending on size/occupancy. Profit margins 30-60%; cap rates ~6.5-8%. These are industry averages skewed by facility size, not a verified median. Smaller rural single-facility owners earn far less.
frequency: ~52,000+ US facilities (StorTrack tracks ~67,000); ~65% owned by small operators outside the top 100.
barrierToEntry: $1M-$5M+ to buy/build a facility (real estate); the most capital-intensive deck here, but most passive.

name: Express Tunnel Car Wash
whatYouDo: Own an express exterior tunnel wash with unlimited-membership subscriptions -- recurring revenue, high fixed cost, low marginal cost per car. Best sites push 200K+ cars/yr.
incomeRangeNote: Industry-wide average revenue/establishment only ~$250K (dominated by tiny operators), but a well-located EXPRESS tunnel grosses $1.5M-$4M/yr at 35-50% operating margins (45-67% adjusted cash-flow margins). Single-site owner income commonly $150K-$500K+. Figures are industry estimates; wide spread by format.
frequency: ~57,900 US car wash & detailing businesses (IBISWorld 2024), up ~2% YoY; express tunnels are a fast-growing subset.
barrierToEntry: $3M-$7M to build a single express tunnel (land + equipment); very capital-intensive.

name: Vending Machine Route
whatYouDo: Build and service a route of snack/beverage vending machines in offices, gyms, and high-traffic sites. Negotiate placements, restock, and collect; reinvest cash flow into more machines.
incomeRangeNote: Per machine: $150-$400/mo gross, $40-$120 net (25-35% margins). A 15-machine route ~$5,500/mo net (~$66K/yr) part-time; first-year 3-5 machine operators net $3,500-$8,000/yr. Median active operator ~$30K-$50K; figures are operator self-reports/industry estimates.
frequency: ~2M machines in use, operated by ~17,600 vending businesses; ~$7B+ industry, 18,000+ operators.
barrierToEntry: $2K-$10K per machine; start with a few machines and bootstrap -- lowest hard-capital entry of the physical-asset decks.

name: ATM Route
whatYouDo: Place independently owned ATMs in bars, convenience stores, and high-cash venues; earn the surcharge + interchange per withdrawal. Vault cash, monitor remotely, split revenue with the location.
incomeRangeNote: Per machine: ~150-300 withdrawals/mo at $2.50-$4.00 surcharge -> $250-$450/mo net after processing/share; strong placements $400-$600/mo. A 5-machine route ~$30K+/yr; reinvested operators run 20-40 machines at $7K-$15K/mo. Routes trade at 20-30x monthly net. Self-reported industry figures.
frequency: Hundreds of thousands of independently owned ATMs nationwide; highly fragmented, no public operator registry.
barrierToEntry: $2K-$8K per machine + vault cash float; capital is the float, not the hardware.

name: HVAC / Plumbing Home-Services Company
whatYouDo: Run a residential HVAC and/or plumbing contractor -- techs, trucks, dispatch, maintenance-agreement recurring revenue. The classic 'boring business' rollup target for PE/search funds.
incomeRangeNote: Owner take-home is net-profit-dependent: typical owners $70K-$150K, top owners $200K+; reported national average owner 'salary' ~$86K. Net margins ~8% average (range 8-40%). On $1M revenue, owner take-home often just $25K-$50K after wages -- scale and maintenance contracts drive the upside. Self-reported/benchmark data.
frequency: Tens of thousands of US contractors; heavily fragmented, prime consolidation target.
barrierToEntry: Trade license + 1-2 yrs trade experience; can start with one truck (~$50K-$150K) or buy an existing book.

name: Landscaping / Lawn-Care Company
whatYouDo: Operate a lawn-maintenance and landscape-install business -- recurring mowing routes plus higher-margin design/hardscape/irrigation jobs. Scale crews and route density.
incomeRangeNote: Owner pay typically $54K-$115K; 90th-percentile owners ~$293K, low-end/part-time ~$25K. Net margins 5-20% (maintenance) up to higher on design/install. Owners often take 20-35% of net profit. Self-reported/benchmark; no public median.
frequency: Very large, fragmented industry (hundreds of thousands of US lawn/landscape firms); a top rollup category.
barrierToEntry: Low: a truck, mower/trailer, and a few accounts ($10K-$50K); the easiest physical-services deck to bootstrap.

name: Pressure Washing Business
whatYouDo: Run exterior cleaning -- driveways, houses, commercial flatwork, fleet washing. High per-job margins, low equipment cost; scale with crews and recurring commercial contracts.
incomeRangeNote: Solo/part-time $20K-$30K; full-time owner-operators $60K-$100K; multi-crew/commercial $150K-$250K+. Margins 40-50% (per-job gross 70-80%). Every business reporting >$50K revenue had at least one employee. Self-reported industry estimates.
frequency: Tens of thousands of small operators; extremely low barrier means high churn and a long tail of part-timers.
barrierToEntry: Lowest hard-capital deck: a gas pressure washer + truck (~$2K-$10K) and basic insurance.

name: Pool Service Route
whatYouDo: Own a recurring pool-maintenance route -- weekly cleaning/chemical service at a fixed monthly fee per pool, plus higher-margin repairs. Routes are bought and sold like assets.
incomeRangeNote: Solo owner with 60 accounts at ~$180/mo grosses ~$130K, nets ~$55K-$75K; well-run routes net $80K-$100K+; 150-pool single-tech operations reach six figures. Net margins 15-25% after owner pay. Repairs add 15-30% of revenue. Industry estimates.
frequency: ~125,000 US pool-service businesses; ~$2.52B direct-service market. Highly fragmented.
barrierToEntry: Low: truck, equipment, chemicals, and a purchased route ($30K-$80K for a starter book) or build from scratch.

name: Dental Practice Owner
whatYouDo: Own a private dental practice -- treat patients as the producing dentist AND keep the business profit. Buy an existing practice or start de novo; hire associates to scale beyond your own chair.
incomeRangeNote: ADA Survey of Dental Practice: average net income for general-dentist practice owners ~$215,320; ALL owners (incl. specialists) ~$258,260 vs employed dentists ~$191,150; specialists ~$346,520. Solo practitioners ~$221,490. Collections $800K-$1.5M, overhead 50-70%. Authoritative ADA survey data.
frequency: ~135,665 dental establishments in the US (Census County Business Patterns 2023, NAICS 621210).
barrierToEntry: DDS/DMD degree (4 yrs post-grad) + ~$500K-$1M+ to buy/build a practice; high education AND capital barrier.

name: Medical Practice Owner (Physician)
whatYouDo: Own a private medical practice (e.g., primary care or a specialty clinic) -- bill insurance for your own production plus the practice's profit, employ NPs/PAs and staff to leverage your panel.
incomeRangeNote: A primary-care physician generates ~$1.4M in downstream revenue and bills ~$500K in reimbursements; practice overhead 50-60%, net margin ~15%. Physician-owner income spans ~$214K-$296K for family medicine and far higher for procedural specialties. No single public median; figures are benchmark estimates.
frequency: Tens of thousands of physician-owned practices, but the share is shrinking fast as hospitals/PE acquire them.
barrierToEntry: MD/DO + residency (~11+ yrs total) + license; plus capital to buy/start a practice. Highest education barrier here.

name: Amazon FBA / E-Commerce Store
whatYouDo: Source or private-label products, ship to Amazon's fulfillment network, and sell via the marketplace; Amazon handles storage, picking, and delivery. Scale by finding winning SKUs and reinvesting.
incomeRangeNote: Jungle Scout 2024: new sellers average ~$30K/yr profit; 40% do $1K-$25K/mo in SALES (not profit); margins ~15-20% net. 58% profitable within year one; 71% work <20 hrs/wk. Wide right-skewed distribution -- $30K is a new-seller average, top sellers far higher. Survey self-report.
frequency: Millions of third-party Amazon sellers worldwide; hundreds of thousands of active US FBA businesses.
barrierToEntry: Lowest-capital online deck: a few thousand dollars of inventory + a laptop; no license, location, or staff.

name: YouTube channel (ad + sponsor creator)
whatYouDo: Build a YouTube channel for ads, sponsors, and (for the real winners) an ecosystem of products and owned channels. AdSense alone is a weak career; the meta deck is YouTube-as-business. Median stays near zero because most channels never monetize.
incomeRangeNote: BRUTAL: the median person who STARTS a channel earns ~$0 (never hits monetization threshold). Among the ~3M channels actually IN the Partner Program, a commonly cited median is ~$4,800/yr, with top 10% ~$42K+/yr. ~89% of channels at 1,000 subs earn nothing meaningful from ads. Finance/B2B niches pay multiples of entertainment RPM. All figures self-reported/aggregator-modeled. YouTube does not publish per-channel income.
frequency: 3M+ channels in YouTube Partner Program (2026, up from 2M in 2022); billions of channels exist, so monetized channels are a tiny fraction
barrierToEntry: $0 + camera/phone; real barrier is skill (editing, on-camera, thumbnails) and 1-3 yrs of consistent output to escape the zero-revenue floor

name: Twitch / live-streaming
whatYouDo: Stream live (gaming, IRL, music) for hours daily, build a concurrent-viewer base, monetize via subs ($2.50 net/sub), bits, ads, and, the real income, sponsorships and direct donations. Brutally winner-take-all and time-intensive (often 40+ hrs/week live with no guaranteed pay).
incomeRangeNote: BRUTAL: median streamer earns near $0 from platform payouts. The 2021 Twitch payout leak showed you'd need to be in the top ~0.015% of streamers to clear US median household income from direct Twitch revenue. ~1,000th-ranked streamer ~$85K/yr; top accounts millions. Leak excludes sponsorships/direct donations (so it understates top earners and overstates how broad the money is). Verifiable but dated (2019-2021 window).
frequency: Millions of active channels; only a few thousand earn a living wage from platform payouts
barrierToEntry: $0-500 (decent mic/cam/PC); barrier is thousands of hours live before any audience forms, plus on-stream charisma

name: Newsletter / Substack
whatYouDo: Write a recurring email newsletter, grow free subscribers, convert a slice (~3-10%) to paid (~$5-10/mo); Substack takes 10% + Stripe fees. Income scales with paid-sub count. The hard part is distribution, nearly all top earners arrived with a pre-existing audience.
incomeRangeNote: BRUTAL: most newsletters have zero paid subs and earn $0. Among the ~17,000+ writers who DO get paid, a cited median is ~$4,000/yr and nearly 50% of those with paid subs earned under $500 in 2025. Top 10% captures ~62% of all payments; 50+ publications clear $1M/yr, almost all pre-existing public figures. Platform-level revenue is real; per-writer figures are aggregator/self-reported.
frequency: 17,000+ writers earning money on Substack; collectively ~$450M gross in 2025
barrierToEntry: $0 (free to start); barrier is an audience and a reason for people to pay, writing skill alone is insufficient

name: Podcasting
whatYouDo: Record and publish episodes on a cadence, grow downloads, monetize via dynamic ad insertion (CPM-based), host-read sponsorships, and memberships/Patreon. Ad income is roughly downloads-driven, so it's brutally gated on audience size. Most shows quit before monetizing.
incomeRangeNote: BRUTAL: most podcasters make $0-$500/mo; the median show earns essentially nothing. Ad money meaningfully starts around ~10,000 downloads/episode (~$500-900/episode), which very few reach. ~4M+ indexed podcasts are inactive ('podfade'), many quitting before episode 10. Only the top ~1% clear six figures. Figures are industry-survey/aggregator, not audited.
frequency: Millions of podcasts indexed; 4M+ already inactive (podfade); only a small slice actively monetized
barrierToEntry: $100-500 (mic + hosting); barrier is consistency and reaching the ~10K-download threshold where ads pay

name: Indie / micro-SaaS
whatYouDo: Build a small software product solving a narrow problem, charge recurring subscriptions (MRR), and grow via SEO/content/communities. Solo or tiny team. The grind is distribution and churn, not coding. Most products are abandoned before reaching ramen profitability.
incomeRangeNote: BRUTAL and survivorship-heavy: across 1,000+ studied micro-SaaS, the median is ~$500/mo MRR (~$6K/yr) and ~70% of founders never break $1,000/mo; ~40% never reach $1K MRR. Indie Hackers showcases winners, heavy survivor bias. Only ~18% reach the $1K-$5K/mo 'pay-the-bills' zone. Numbers are self-reported by founders who choose to disclose.
frequency: Tens of thousands of indie founders; only low thousands sustain a full-time income
barrierToEntry: $0-100/mo infra + coding ability (or AI-assisted build); real barrier is months of unpaid building + marketing skill

name: Freelancing (Upwork/Fiverr/contract dev, design, writing)
whatYouDo: Sell a skill (development, design, writing, marketing) by the hour or project on marketplaces (Upwork/Fiverr) or direct contracts. Income = rate x billable hours, so it's capped by time and gated on landing clients and reviews. The most reliable of these decks, but you trade time for money with no leverage.
incomeRangeNote: Bimodal. On marketplaces most accounts earn near $0, an oft-cited Upwork stat is ~1 in 800 freelancers makes $1,000+/mo on the platform. BUT established/full-time freelancers do far better: Upwork's own figure puts median full-time freelancer income ~$85K (2024, self-reported survey), with dev ~$21-55/hr and ML eng ~$100/hr median. Marketplace-tail data partial; income survey self-reported.
frequency: Tens of millions of freelancer accounts globally; a small fraction earn meaningful platform income
barrierToEntry: $0 + a marketable skill; barrier is the skill itself and cold-start (no reviews = no clients)

name: Dropshipping
whatYouDo: Run a storefront (Shopify) selling products you don't stock; supplier ships direct. You make money on the spread between ad-driven sale price and product+ad cost. The entire game is paid-ad math and margin, most stores spend more on ads than they earn back.
incomeRangeNote: BRUTAL: ~80-90%+ of stores fail within the first year; commonly cited that only ~10-20% reach sustained profitability and only ~1.5% exceed $50K/mo revenue (revenue, not profit). Median store earns $0 net. Established survivors average ~$1K-5K/mo with ~10-30% margins. 'Failure rate' stats are noisy because many counted stores were never seriously run. Self-reported/aggregator.
frequency: Millions of stores launched; vast majority abandoned within months
barrierToEntry: Low cash ($0-500 for store + a starter ad budget), but you MUST fund paid ads to test; real barrier is ad-buying skill and working capital to absorb losing tests

name: Print-on-demand (Merch by Amazon / Etsy / Redbubble)
whatYouDo: Upload designs onto blank products (shirts, mugs); the platform prints/ships on each order and pays a royalty. You compete on design + niche + listing SEO. Volume game with tiny per-unit margins; most designs never sell.
incomeRangeNote: BRUTAL: most uploaders earn $0, typically only ~15-25% of designs make ANY sale and ~5-10% of designs drive most income. Median Etsy shop is cited around ~$574/mo (all categories, not just POD), with the elite 1% near $50K/mo. Royalties are thin (~$2-7/shirt on Amazon Merch). Per-seller figures are aggregator/self-reported; platforms don't publish seller income.
frequency: Hundreds of thousands to millions of seller accounts; tiny fraction earn a living
barrierToEntry: $0 (no inventory); barrier is design/niche skill and the volume of listings + SEO needed to get discovered

name: Online courses / cohorts
whatYouDo: Package expertise into a video course (Udemy/Teachable) or live cohort, then sell it. Income = price x students, so it's gated entirely on audience and trust. On marketplaces like Udemy you also surrender pricing power to platform discounting. Most courses get a handful of students.
incomeRangeNote: BRUTAL: ~75% of Udemy instructors make under $1,000/yr; median instructor cited ~$4,200/yr; only ~1% earn a full-time (>$50K) income and the top 1% take >50% of all earnings. ~50% of courses created since 2020 have under 100 lifetime enrollments. Self-hosted cohorts can do better per-student but need an existing audience. Aggregator/self-reported.
frequency: Hundreds of thousands of course creators on Udemy alone; full-time earners are ~1%
barrierToEntry: $0 + real teachable expertise and recording effort; barrier is audience/distribution and standing out in a saturated catalog

name: Affiliate / SEO content sites
whatYouDo: Build a content site ranking in Google for buyer-intent keywords, then earn commissions on links to products (Amazon Associates, networks). Income = traffic x conversion x commission. Brutally dependent on Google's algorithm, one core update can erase your income overnight.
incomeRangeNote: BRUTAL: ~9% of affiliates earn under $1,000/yr and ~57% earn under $10K/yr; only ~11% clear $100K+. Survey medians for ACTIVE affiliates land ~$500-$2,000/mo, but that excludes the huge tail earning ~$0. Headline 'average ~$8K/mo' is skewed by whales, do not treat as typical. Survey-based and self-reported; also exposed to Google update risk.
frequency: Millions attempt affiliate/SEO; majority of sites never rank or earn
barrierToEntry: Low cash ($50-200/yr hosting+domain); barrier is SEO skill and 6-18 months of content before traffic (and income) arrive, if it ranks at all

name: Marketing agency (owner-operator)
whatYouDo: Sell a marketing service (ads, SEO, social, content) to businesses on retainer; deliver yourself, then hire as you scale. Income is client retainers minus delivery cost. More durable than creator decks because B2B clients pay real money, but year 1 owners often take little or no distribution.
incomeRangeNote: Owner take is highly stage-dependent: little/no distributions in year 1, breakeven ~year 2, meaningful payouts by ~year 5. Niched year-1 founders cited at ~$5K-$30K/mo REVENUE (not owner pay), scaling to $30K-$100K/mo by years 2-3. Net margins ~13-22% for sub-8-figure agencies. Many agencies never get past the founder-as-sole-operator stage. Benchmark/self-reported.
frequency: Hundreds of thousands of small/solo agencies; high churn, many stay one-person shops
barrierToEntry: $0 cash but HIGH skill bar, you must already be good at the service AND at sales; real barrier is client acquisition

name: App development (indie mobile/App Store)
whatYouDo: Build and ship a mobile app, monetize via subscriptions, IAP, or ads, and fight for visibility in a store of millions. Apple/Google take 15-30%. Brutal power-law: a tiny number of apps capture almost all revenue; most never get downloaded.
incomeRangeNote: BRUTAL: ~94% of app developers earn under $1,000/mo and the top ~1% captures 90%+ of store revenue; the honest solo-indie median is under $1,000/mo (effectively ~$0 for most). Top-quartile solo result ~$3K-$15K/mo after 12-18 months with tight niche + subscriptions. Top 0.1% of apps take >half of all IAP revenue. Aggregator/self-reported; platforms don't publish per-developer income.
frequency: Millions of developers and apps; livable income confined to a small top slice
barrierToEntry: $99/yr Apple dev (+$25 Google) + real coding skill; barrier is engineering ability plus the much harder problem of distribution/ASO

name: B2B SaaS Sales (AE)
whatYouDo: Sell software to businesses: prospect, demo, close. SDR → Account Executive → Enterprise AE. Pay is base + uncapped commission (OTE).
incomeRangeNote: BLS technical-sales median $99,710; tech AE on-target earnings commonly $150K-$400K with commission (self-reported).
frequency: ~1.9M technical & wholesale sales reps (BLS)
barrierToEntry: No degree required; weeks of ramp; learn the product + a sales process.

name: Big-Tech SWE (equity comp)
whatYouDo: Software engineering at a top-tier company where total comp = base + RSUs + bonus. The real high-ROI tech deck hidden inside the $133K 'software developer' median.
incomeRangeNote: levels.fyi total comp $250K-$700K+ at senior; self-reported and survivorship-tilted toward those who pass the interview bar.
frequency: Subset of the ~1.7M developers (BLS)
barrierToEntry: Strong CS fundamentals + months of interview prep; bachelor's typical, no license; $0 capital.

name: Real Estate Agent / Broker
whatYouDo: Help people buy and sell property for commission. License in weeks; income is commission and highly bimodal, top producers and team leads scale far above the median.
incomeRangeNote: BLS median $56,620 (agents); brokers ~$63K; top producers six-to-seven figures while many earn little.
barrierToEntry: Weeks of licensing; ~$1-2K start; near-zero capital.

name: Recruiter / Staffing Agency
whatYouDo: Place candidates into jobs for a 15-25% fee. Solo desk or build an agency; phone + laptop business.
incomeRangeNote: BLS recruiter base ~$67K; agency/contingent and exec-search recruiters clear $150K-$500K (partly self-reported).
frequency: ~900K HR specialists/recruiters (BLS)
barrierToEntry: No capital; learn sourcing + sales; no license.

name: Fractional CFO / Bookkeeping
whatYouDo: Run finances for several small businesses on retainer, recurring B2B revenue. Bookkeeping needs no license; fractional CFO leverages accounting depth.
incomeRangeNote: BLS accountants/auditors median $79,880; solo bookkeepers $50-100K, fractional CFOs $150K-$300K+ (partial).
frequency: ~1.5M accountants & auditors (BLS)
barrierToEntry: Accounting skill (CPA optional; bookkeeping needs none); low capital; recurring clients.

name: AI Automation Agency
whatYouDo: Build AI agents, RAG, and workflow automation for SMBs as a project or retainer service. Sister deck to service-as-software: here you still sell hours/projects; there you sell a productized outcome. Fast 2025-26 entry niche, skill-gated.
incomeRangeNote: No official data; project fees vary widely; income is highly variable and self-reported. Skill-gated, zero capital, days-to-weeks to first client. Many shops stay under ramen profitability.
frequency: Emerging (unmeasured)
barrierToEntry: Skill-gated (AI + integration); $0 capital; days-weeks to first client.

name: Med Spa / Aesthetic Injector (owner)
whatYouDo: RN/NP-fronted aesthetics clinic (Botox, fillers, lasers). High-margin, cash-pay, one of the fastest-growing healthcare-adjacent businesses.
incomeRangeNote: Owner income varies widely; high-margin cash-pay; ceiling far above the salaried NP deck. Partly self-reported.
frequency: Fast-growing segment
barrierToEntry: RN/NP (or MD medical director) + clinic buildout capital ($100K-$500K).

name: Short-Term-Rental Arbitrage / Co-hosting
whatYouDo: Run Airbnb units you lease (arbitrage) or co-host others' properties for a cut, little or no property ownership.
incomeRangeNote: Self-reported; co-hosting ~10-20% of revenue; arbitrage margins thin with real platform and regulatory risk.
frequency: Many small operators (unmeasured)
barrierToEntry: Low capital (deposits + furnishing); operational + sales skill; regulatory risk.

name: Productized service
whatYouDo: Package a freelance skill into a fixed-scope, fixed-price offer (or flat monthly retainer) sold the same way every time. DesignJoy-style: one menu, no custom quotes. The ladder off freelancing toward an agency or SaaS.
incomeRangeNote: No public median. Solo productized shops that stick often clear more than pure freelancing because pricing is standardized and delivery is templatized, but most freelancers never productize and stay in the zero-to-low band. Self-reported exemplars only.
frequency: Growing subset of freelancers and solo agencies; unmeasured at scale
barrierToEntry: Must already sell a skill clients pay for; zero capital; real barrier is productizing scope and running a sales system

name: Lead generation sites
whatYouDo: Build niche content/landing sites that capture buyer intent (insurance, legal, home services, education) and sell leads to local or national buyers at a higher RPL than your CPL. Classic high-margin internet arbitrage.
incomeRangeNote: No public operator median. Successful lead-gen sites can run 50-80%+ net margins once traffic is stable, but most never convert enough volume and paid-traffic tests burn cash. Buyer market (Flippa/FE) shows winners; survivors only.
frequency: Tens of thousands of niche lead sites; livable full-time operators are a small fraction
barrierToEntry: Low cash for content SEO path; paid-traffic path needs working capital for tests. Real barrier is conversion math and vertical relationships.

name: Digital products (templates / tools)
whatYouDo: Sell non-course digital goods once: Notion templates, design kits, prompt packs, spreadsheets, UI kits, ebooks, small tools. Content is distribution; the product is the monetization layer (Gumroad/Lemon Squeezy style).
incomeRangeNote: Platform averages are tiny; most listings earn near zero. Winners with distribution hit five to seven figures. Stan/Gumroad-style marketplaces show long-tail power laws, not livable medians. Self-reported.
frequency: Hundreds of thousands of digital product listings; full-time earners are a thin top slice
barrierToEntry: Zero cash to list; barrier is having something people already search for and an audience or SEO path to it

name: Community / membership
whatYouDo: Charge recurring dues for access to a private community, office hours, peer group, or members-only content (Circle, Discord, Mighty Networks, custom). Owned media with stickier cash than ads.
incomeRangeNote: Circle 2026 community data spans pre-revenue to multi-million; most communities stay small or never monetize. Recurring when it works; churn and content load when it does not. Self-reported / partial platform surveys.
frequency: Hundreds of thousands of creator communities; monetized full-time ones are far fewer
barrierToEntry: 0-50 USD/mo platform fee; barrier is an audience that trusts you and a reason to pay monthly

name: Short-form content (funnel only)
whatYouDo: Use TikTok, Reels, or Shorts as top-of-funnel discovery. Platform payouts are weak; the deck only scores when clips push traffic into email, YouTube depth, a product, or a service. Treat it as a card in a hybrid, not a career.
incomeRangeNote: Native payouts often about 0.40-1.00 USD per 1k views. Brand deals pay at scale but are inconsistent. Median full-time income from short-form alone is effectively near zero for the mass of posters. Self-reported / industry estimates.
frequency: Tens of millions of posters; full-time income from the platform alone is rare
barrierToEntry: Zero cash + phone; barrier is consistency, editing skill, and a real monetization layer behind the clip

name: UGC / clipping / creator-for-hire
whatYouDo: Make short ads, product clips, or repurposed content for brands and creators for a fee. 2025-26 entry meta for people who can edit fast. Ladder into a UGC agency or productized content shop.
incomeRangeNote: Gig rates vary wildly (tens of dollars per clip to retainers). No BLS series. Many treat it as side income; full-time requires volume or higher-ticket retainers. Self-reported.
frequency: Fast-growing gig category; unmeasured formally
barrierToEntry: 0-500 USD gear; barrier is portfolio proof and outbound to brands/creators

name: High-ticket consulting
whatYouDo: Sell advice, strategy, or done-with-you work at high price points (often 2k-20k+ USD) to a narrow niche. Small audience, high LTV. Distinct from mass courses: fewer clients, deeper engagement.
incomeRangeNote: No public median. Works when you already have proven outcomes buyers will pay for. Zero without trust, distribution, or sales skill. Self-reported ceilings look huge; typical attempts earn nothing.
frequency: Large long tail of coaches/consultants; full-time six-figure operators are a small share
barrierToEntry: Skill and proof, not capital. Real gate is a niche that pays and a sales process.

name: Chrome extension / tiny tools
whatYouDo: Ship a focused browser extension or tiny utility that solves one workflow pain. Monetize via freemium, one-time purchase, or low MRR. Cousin of micro-SaaS with a sharper distribution surface (Chrome Web Store).
incomeRangeNote: Most extensions never monetize. A thin top slice (indie tools, SEO utilities, productivity) can clear full-time income. Same power-law as micro-SaaS. Self-reported.
frequency: Hundreds of thousands of extensions; few earn a living wage
barrierToEntry: Coding skill (or AI-assisted) + low store fees; distribution and trust reviews are the hard part

name: Email list / info funnel
whatYouDo: Own the relationship: grow an email list, nurture with free value, sell digital products, services, or affiliate offers. The glue deck in most hybrid stacks (short-form or SEO in, offer out).
incomeRangeNote: List size without an offer is worthless. Operators with a list + offer can out-earn larger free audiences. No honest public median; near-zero for people who only collect emails. Self-reported.
frequency: Millions of newsletters and lists; monetized funnels are far fewer
barrierToEntry: 0-30 USD/mo ESP; barrier is a lead magnet people want and something worth selling

name: Service-as-software (outcome retainers)
whatYouDo: Sell the outcome, not the tool: AI agents, automation, and human-in-the-loop systems priced as monthly results (leads booked, tickets closed, invoices recovered). 2026 evolution of the pure AI automation agency.
incomeRangeNote: Emerging category with no official wage series. Closest relatives are agencies and micro-SaaS. Early winners report strong retainers; most shops stay project-based or die. Self-reported only. Treat big claims skeptically.
frequency: Emerging; unmeasured. Growing fast in indie and agency circles 2025-26
barrierToEntry: AI + integration skill + sales; zero capital; days to weeks to first pilot if you can sell

name: Ghostwriting / copywriting
whatYouDo: Write for others under their name or brand: books, newsletters, LinkedIn, sales pages, ads. Pure skill trade with high rates when you niche (SaaS, finance, health).
incomeRangeNote: No public median. Strong freelancers clear 50k-150k+; most marketplace writers earn little. Self-reported.
frequency: Large freelance writing pool; full-time ghostwriters are a smaller skilled slice
barrierToEntry: Writing skill + samples; zero capital; niche proof is the gate

name: Virtual assistant
whatYouDo: Remote admin, inbox, calendar, research, light ops for founders and creators. Classic ladder into specialized ops or an agency.
incomeRangeNote: Often 15-40 USD/hr online; full-time retainers exist but median platform income is low. Self-reported.
frequency: Millions of VA profiles globally; livable retainers are a thin slice
barrierToEntry: Organization + tools fluency; zero capital; portfolio and reliability win

name: Social media management
whatYouDo: Plan, create, schedule, and report social content for brands. Retainer-friendly if you productize a niche.
incomeRangeNote: Agency and freelance rates vary; many underprice. No BLS series for solo SMMs. Self-reported.
frequency: Very common side and full-time offer; high competition
barrierToEntry: Platform skill + samples; zero capital; client acquisition is the hard part

name: SEO specialist (freelance)
whatYouDo: Rank sites via technical SEO, content, and links. B2B retainers; AI search is changing tactics but demand remains.
incomeRangeNote: Specialists with proof clear strong retainers; beginners struggle. Self-reported.
frequency: Crowded service niche; competent operators scarcer than profiles
barrierToEntry: SEO skill + case studies; tools budget optional; results proof is the gate

name: No-code / AI web builds
whatYouDo: Ship client sites and light apps with Webflow, Framer, Bubble, or AI builders. Fast delivery, fixed packages.
incomeRangeNote: Project fees 1k-15k+ common in marketing; race-to-bottom on Fiverr. Self-reported.
frequency: Fast-growing service offer post-AI builders
barrierToEntry: Tool fluency + taste; zero capital; sales and revisions management matter

name: Amazon KDP / self-publishing
whatYouDo: Publish ebooks or paperbacks on Amazon KDP (fiction, nonfiction, low-content). Ads and catalog size drive outcomes.
incomeRangeNote: Power law: most titles earn near zero; a thin top slice lives on catalogs + ads. Self-reported.
frequency: Millions of KDP titles; full-time authors are rare
barrierToEntry: Writing or outsourcing; low cash; advertising skill separates winners

name: TikTok Shop / social commerce
whatYouDo: Sell products in-feed via TikTok Shop or similar social commerce. Content is the storefront; margins and returns matter.
incomeRangeNote: Viral outliers dominate narratives; most sellers do not clear full-time income. Self-reported / platform opaque.
frequency: Large and growing seller base on social commerce platforms
barrierToEntry: Content skill + product source; ad/sample budget helps; algorithm risk is real

name: Online reselling / retail arbitrage
whatYouDo: Buy underpriced goods (retail, thrift, clearance, wholesale) and resell on Amazon, eBay, or Poshmark. Ops-heavy.
incomeRangeNote: Part-time side income common; full-time needs capital velocity and discipline. Self-reported.
frequency: Very large long tail of resellers
barrierToEntry: Working capital for inventory; sourcing skill; platform account risk

name: Domain flipping
whatYouDo: Buy brandable or aged domains and resell via marketplaces. Illiquid, knowledge-heavy, lottery-like tops.
incomeRangeNote: Most portfolios sit unsold; a few sales can make a year. Not a reliable salary path. Self-reported.
frequency: Thousands of active flippers; many hobbyists
barrierToEntry: Capital for names + market taste; patience for sales cycles

name: Online tutoring
whatYouDo: Teach subjects or test prep over Zoom (platforms or direct). Time-for-money with high hourly when credentialed.
incomeRangeNote: Platform rates often modest; private tutoring higher. Partial public data by subject. Self-reported overall.
frequency: Large global tutoring marketplace
barrierToEntry: Subject mastery + teaching skill; zero capital; credentials help premium rates

name: Bug bounty / security research
whatYouDo: Find vulnerabilities for bounty platforms (HackerOne, Bugcrowd) or contract pentests. Skill-gated, variable payouts.
incomeRangeNote: Top hunters earn a lot; most accounts earn little or nothing. Extreme power law. Self-reported.
frequency: Tens of thousands of researchers; few full-time on bounties alone
barrierToEntry: Deep security skill; zero capital; reputation and methodology matter

name: Faceless content channels
whatYouDo: Run YouTube/TikTok channels without showing your face (narration, compilations, AI-assisted). Still needs distribution craft.
incomeRangeNote: Hyped path; CPM and copyright risks; median near zero like other attention decks. Self-reported.
frequency: Crowded post-AI; many abandoned channels
barrierToEntry: Editing + niche selection; low capital; originality and rights issues are the trap

name: Surveys / microtasks
whatYouDo: Complete surveys, user tests, or microtasks (mTurk-class). Pocket money, not a career. On the board for honesty.
incomeRangeNote: Typical earnings are a few dollars per hour. Not a livable path for almost anyone. Verifiable via platform economics.
frequency: Tens of millions try; almost none live on it
barrierToEntry: Account + internet; zero skill gate; the win rate is the problem

name: Email marketing (service)
whatYouDo: Write and run email for brands: flows, campaigns, list growth. High ROI channel; retainer-friendly when productized.
incomeRangeNote: Specialists with ecom/SaaS case studies price well; generalists compete down. Self-reported.
frequency: Growing service niche tied to ecommerce and creators
barrierToEntry: Copy + ESP fluency; zero capital; proof of lifts is the sales weapon

name: Stock media / asset sales
whatYouDo: Sell photos, video clips, audio, LUTs, or 3D assets on stock marketplaces. Volume + niches.
incomeRangeNote: Long tail near zero; catalogs compound slowly. Self-reported.
frequency: Millions of contributors across stock platforms
barrierToEntry: Gear or skill; low capital on phone photography; volume required

name: Remote bookkeeping
whatYouDo: Books for small businesses remotely (QBO/Xero). Close cousin to fractional CFO with lower barrier.
incomeRangeNote: Many bookkeepers land 40-70k equivalent via clients; partial BLS adjacency to bookkeeping occupations.
frequency: Large small-business demand; many solo practitioners
barrierToEntry: Bookkeeping skill; software certs help; zero capital; recurring clients are the prize

name: Freelance web development
whatYouDo: Build sites and web apps for clients as a solo dev or tiny studio. Distinct from productized no-code and full-time SWE.
incomeRangeNote: Rates span 30-200+ USD/hr by skill. Strong operators clear six figures; marketplaces compress juniors. Self-reported.
frequency: Huge freelance dev market globally
barrierToEntry: Coding skill; zero capital; portfolio and sales beat credentials

name: Influencer / brand deals
whatYouDo: Monetize an audience via sponsorships and brand deals across IG/TikTok/YouTube. Reach engine with uneven payouts.
incomeRangeNote: Most creators earn little from deals; mid-tier can work; mega outliers dominate press. Self-reported surveys.
frequency: Hundreds of millions identify as creators; paid influencers are a tiny fraction
barrierToEntry: Audience first; zero capital; authenticity and niche brand fit matter
